danger_signals.meaning: Indicates hazardous zones requiring avoidance or combat

danger_signals.visual_form: Historical era-specific obstacles and traps in Amazatorium levels
danger_signals.meaning: World-state danger tied to current theme park zone (temporal/historical setting)

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual health indicator decreasing after taking hits
danger_signals.meaning: Player is in danger; three hits available before losing a life

safety_signals.meaning: Safe to collect; indicates progression and resource gathering

safety_signals.visual_form: Nerf Ballzooka gun and other special equipment
safety_signals.meaning: Enhanced player capabilities; temporary safety advantage

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Bubsy character sprite and family imagery
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Levels accessible to player; safe zones with allies (niece Terri, nephew Terry, sidekick Arnold)
Amazatorium theme park structure; historical era-based levels where stolen items/information are hidden
Multi-era themed environments (ancient, futuristic, historical periods); level selection hub

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Oinker's theft in progress / historical timeline corruption
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Amazatorium displays stolen historical items; virtual world recreations of different historical eras
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy placement and level design reflect era-specific threats; time machine concept manifests as shifting environmental hazards

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Amazatorium interior with themed zones representing different historical periods
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Non-linear stage selection indicates player freedom to explore any era; security/enemy patrols imply break-in context

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Nerf Ballzooka availability; marble collection UI
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Ability to shoot and traverse increases; three-hit tolerance allows player agency in combat encounters

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Presence of Terri and Terry characters within theme park levels
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Driving narrative motivation; environmental storytelling through character placement in dangerous zones

environmental_storytelling.example: Each Amazatorium level represents a distinct historical era, visually communicating world-state of stolen historical artifacts on display
Bubsy's niece and nephew are positioned throughout the park levels, indicating they triggered the infiltration and need rescue
Oinker's influence visible through level design choices and enemy deployment patterns reflecting his theme park structure
Non-linear level progression allows players to choose historical eras to explore, communicating open-ended world exploration vs. linear narrative rails

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Jumping and gliding through platforming challenges; collecting marbles
Equipped with Nerf Ballzooka; can shoot enemies and environmental targets
Takes up to three hits before losing a life; visual health feedback
Positioned as obstacles throughout levels; require avoidance or destruction via Ballzooka
Located within theme park; motivation for Bubsy's infiltration and rescue mission
Accompanies Bubsy on rescue mission; provides assistance throughout levels

notes: Bubsy II communicates world-state primarily through thematic level design and environmental setting (Amazatorium with historical era-based zones). Political situation is straightforward: corrupt entrepreneur Oinker steals from history via time machine; this villainy is manifested through enemy placement and level architecture rather than complex faction mechanics. The non-linear stage selection system communicates player agency and freedom within a contained space (the theme park). Danger is signaled through enemy presence and era-specific hazards; safety through clear platforming paths and collectibles. The game lacks sophisticated visual faction indicators—instead relying on character presence and environmental framing to establish good vs. evil.
